name: Peña Mesa — circular trailrunning through the Espacio Natural de las Tuerces
name_original: Peña Mesa — circular trailrunning por el Espacio Natural de las Tuerces
region_intro: Jogging in Espacio Natural De Las Tuerces offers a chance to explore a landscape characterized by unique limestone formations, rock shelters, and varied terrain. The region features a mix of open areas and paths winding through natural rock sculptures. Running routes here often include moderate elevation changes, providing a good workout amidst distinctive geological features. The trails are well-suited for those seeking a natural running experience. Best jogging routes in Espacio Natural De Las Tuerces The most popular jogging route is Peña Mesa — circular trailrunning through the Espacio Natural de las Tuerces, a 3.1 miles (5.0 km) trail that takes 41 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the distinctive rock formations. Another top favourite among local runners is Horadada Tunnel – Peña Mesa loop from Olleros de Pisuerga, a moderate 7.4 miles (11.9 km) path. This route traverses varied terrain, including the notable Horadada Tunnel. Local runners also love the Gama Castle – Peña Mesa loop from Espacio Natural de Las Tuerces, a 11.0 miles (17.6 km) trail leading through a landscape of rock formations and natural features, often completed in about 2 hours 13 minutes. Jogging in Espacio Natural De Las Tuerces is defined by limestone karsts, natural rock shelters, and varied trail surfaces. The network offers options for moderate and difficult running, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Espacio Natural De Las Tuerces are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 8 reviews. More than 90 runners have used komoot to explore Espacio Natural De Las Tuerces's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Espacio Natural De Las Tuerces?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;over 20 running routes&lt;/strong&gt; in Espacio Natural De Las Tuerces, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. The area offers a diverse selection of trails to explore.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails in Espacio Natural De Las Tuerces?
A: The terrain in Espacio Natural De Las Tuerces is characterized by unique limestone formations, rock shelters, and varied surfaces. You'll encounter a mix of open areas and paths winding through natural rock sculptures, often with moderate elevation changes.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running routes in the region?
A: While the guide primarily lists moderate to difficult routes, the overall landscape of Espacio Natural De Las Tuerces allows for varied experiences. For a relatively shorter and less strenuous option, consider &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1119394160/pena-mesa-circular-trailrunning-through-the-espacio-natural-de-las-tuerces" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Mesa — circular trailrunning through the Espacio Natural de las Tuerces&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.1 miles (5.0 km) long and offers a moderate challenge.

Q: What are some of the longer running routes available?
A: For a longer run, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31460433" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gama Castle – Peña Mesa loop from Espacio Natural de Las Tuerces&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 11.0 miles (17.6 km) trail. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31455185" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de España – Pisuerga River loop from Aguilar de Campoo&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 7.9 miles (12.7 km).

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Espacio Natural De Las Tuerces?
A: Yes, many of the routes are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40003789" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Horadada Tunnel – Peña Mesa loop from Olleros de Pisuerga&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31456503" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa de la Granja loop from Aguilar de Campoo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see along the running trails?
A: Along the running routes, you can encounter several notable landmarks. These include the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4413098" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Mesa&lt;/a&gt; rock formation, the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4045496" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Horadada Tunnel&lt;/a&gt;, and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4586765" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gama Castle&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5707455" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Juan Bautista in Villaescusa de las Torres&lt;/a&gt; is also a point of interest.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about the trails in Espacio Natural De Las Tuerces?
A: The running routes in Espacio Natural De Las Tuerces are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars. Runners often praise the distinctive geological features, the natural rock sculptures, and the varied terrain that provides a good workout amidst unique scenery.

Q: Is Espacio Natural De Las Tuerces suitable for trail running with dogs?
A: Espacio Natural De Las Tuerces is a natural area, and generally, dogs are welcome on trails, especially if kept on a leash. However, it's always recommended to check local regulations or specific trail signs for any restrictions regarding pets before you go.

Q: Are there options for family-friendly running or walking in the area?
A: While some routes feature moderate elevation, the natural beauty and unique rock formations can make for an engaging experience for families. Shorter, less technical sections of trails, or those around villages like Olleros de Pisuerga, might be more suitable for families looking for a gentle run or walk.

Q: What are the best times of year to go running in Espacio Natural De Las Tuerces?
A: The best times for running in Espacio Natural De Las Tuerces are typically spring and autumn when temperatures are milder and the natural landscape is vibrant. Summer can be pleasant, especially in the mornings or late afternoons, but be mindful of higher temperatures. Winter running is possible, but some paths might be muddy or slippery depending on recent weather.

Q: Are there any facilities like cafes or restrooms near the running routes?
A: Espacio Natural De Las Tuerces is a natural area, so facilities like cafes and dedicated restrooms directly on the trails are limited. It's advisable to plan your run to start or end near villages such as Olleros de Pisuerga or Aguilar de Campoo, where you can find amenities.
tips:
Locality located in the UNESCO World Geopark "Las Loras", in the Protected Space of Las Tuerces.
Several large manor houses are still to be found.

Peña Mesa is an impressive rock formation located in the province of Palencia, within the Las Tuerces Natural Area. This place is known for its curious karst formations, created by erosion in the limestone rock, which offer a unique and spectacular landscape.

waypoints.name: Church of San Juan Bautista, Villaescusa de las Torres
